prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol (C₆H₅OH, 13 atoms) was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard) in the gas phase, with charge 0 and multiplicity 1 for the neutral molecule. Cation and anion states (multiplicity 2 for each) were computed to evaluate the per-atom Fukui indices via Mulliken population analysis in PySCF with density fitting. All three electronic states (N, N+1, N−1) converged successfully. The most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 2.103) is carbon atom 3 (ortho to the hydroxyl group); the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.162) is the hydroxyl oxygen. The dual descriptor shows strong nucleophilic character on oxygen (−1.157) and strong electrophilic character on the ortho carbon (2.032), consistent with the resonance-stabilized reactivity of phenol. Charge conservation is satisfied: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0 and Σ(f⁻) = 1.0. A Fukui reactivity map visualization has been generated. The calculation was reproducible via the recorded CLI invocation and output written to a JSON file.
